task: Flagship expert task: Rail Interlocking Route-Locking Logic Acceptance Review. Reality context: a rail signaling team must accept a revised interlocking table before weekend possession ends. Embedded packet: routes: R1 points=[P12 normal,P18 reverse] opposing=R3 track_circuit_clear=Y flank_protection=Y, R2 points=[P14 reverse] opposing=R4 clear=N flank=Y, R3 opposing=R1 clear=Y flank=N, R4 opposing=R2 clear=Y flank=Y; rules route may clear only if track clear, opposing route locked out, and flank protection present; any missing flank on passenger route is fail; passenger routes R1,R3,R4. Rules and constraints: Build pass/fail table, identify unsafe route combinations, and recommend accept/hold with test cases. Required work: deliver route_locking_review.xlsx + signaling_acceptance_memo.md. The deliverables must include formulas or executable logic, intermediate calculations, row-level citations to the embedded packet, validation checks, uncertainty notes, sensitivity checks around the controlling thresholds, and a final decision table with pass/warn/fail/needs_review labels. Use only the embedded packet. Do not import external data, invent missing values, or hide unresolved assumptions.
